articles.summary: Hebb's postulate: neurons that fire together wire together. Simultaneous pre- and postsynaptic activity strengthens the …
articles.content:
Theory

定義

Hebb's postulate: neurons that fire together wire together. Simultaneous pre- and postsynaptic activity strengthens the synapse. Foundational principle underlying LTP and associative learning. Anti-Hebbian: if only pre fires without post, synapse weakens (LTD).

記憶口訣

💡 Hebb's rule: cells that FIRE together WIRE together. Think of Hebb as the original #relationship goals of neurons.

Assessed by Wada test (amobarbital injection). Planum temporale asymmetry underlies language lateralization.

💡 Left = Language and Logic. Right = Recognition and visuoSpatial. Wada test: inject amobarbital to sleep one hemisphere, test the other.

articles.title: Long-Term Potentiation (LTP)
articles.summary: The cellular mechanism of learning and memory. Repeated synaptic stimulation produces a lasting increase in synaptic str…
articles.content:
Biological Mechanism

定義

The cellular mechanism of learning and memory. Repeated synaptic stimulation produces a lasting increase in synaptic strength. Requires NMDA receptor activation, calcium influx, and AMPA receptor insertion. Discovered in hippocampus by Bliss and Lomo (1973).

記憶口訣

💡 LTP = Learning Turns Persistent. Hebb's rule: neurons that fire together wire together. NMDA is the coincidence detector (needs pre AND post activation).

Legal / Ethical Term

定義

M'Naghten (1843): Not guilty if did not know nature of act OR did not know it was wrong. ALI/Model Penal Code: Lacked substantial capacity to appreciate criminality OR conform conduct to law. IDRA 1984 narrowed standard post-Hinckley.

記憶口訣

💡 M'Naghten = Must Not Know (what you did or that it was wrong). ALI = Appreciate + conform (broader, behaviorally).

Research Method

Measures magnetic fields produced by electrical currents in neurons using SQUID sensors. Millisecond temporal resolution like EEG, but slightly better spatial resolution. Measures tangential (not radial) sources. Good for auditory and somatosensory cortex studies. Very expensive, requires magnetically shielded room.

articles.title: Mirror Neuron System
articles.summary: Neurons in premotor cortex (F5 in monkeys, BA 44 in humans) that fire both when an animal performs an action AND when it…
articles.content:
Theory

定義

Neurons in premotor cortex (F5 in monkeys, BA 44 in humans) that fire both when an animal performs an action AND when it observes another performing the same action. Proposed role in imitation, empathy, language evolution, and theory of mind. Discovered in macaques by Rizzolatti.

記憶口訣

💡 Mirror neurons = Neural empathy. They MIRROR what they see. I watch you reach -> my motor neurons respond AS IF I was reaching.

考試關鍵字

premotor cortex, F5, action observation, imitation, empathy, language evolution, autism, Rizzolatti, motor resonance

相關人物

👤 Rizzolatti, Gallese, Iacoboni

articles.title: Mirror Neurons
articles.summary: Discovered by Rizzolatti in macaque premotor cortex (F5). Neurons fire both when the animal performs an action AND when …
articles.content:
Biological Mechanism

定義

Discovered by Rizzolatti in macaque premotor cortex (F5). Neurons fire both when the animal performs an action AND when it observes the same action performed by another. Proposed role in action understanding, imitation, theory of mind, empathy, and language evolution. Highly debated in humans (evidence indirect via fMRI).

記憶口訣

💡 Mirror neurons MIRROR what you see. Fire when you DO it and when you WATCH it. Your brain rehearses others actions as if they were your own.

考試關鍵字

Rizzolatti, F5, premotor cortex, action understanding, imitation, empathy, theory of mind, language evolution, simulation theory, ASD mirror neuron deficit

articles.title: Neglect Syndrome & Balint Syndrome
articles.summary: Neglect: failure to attend to one side of space (usually left) after right parietal/temporal damage. Patient draws only …
articles.content:
Disorder / Diagnosis

定義

Neglect: failure to attend to one side of space (usually left) after right parietal/temporal damage. Patient draws only right half of objects, ignores left-side food, etc. Balint Syndrome: bilateral parietal damage causing simultanagnosia (see only one object), optic ataxia (misreaching), and ocular apraxia (cannot voluntarily direct gaze).

記憶口訣

💡 Neglect: right parietal damage NEGLECTS the left world. Balint = BALance is gone: can only see ONE object, misreach, lose eye control.

考試關鍵字

right parietal cortex, hemispatial neglect, simultanagnosia, optic ataxia, ocular apraxia, ventral attention network, line cancellation test, representational neglect

相關人物

👤 Bisiach, Corbetta, Mesulam

articles.summary: Failure to attend to contralateral (usually left) space, typically after right parietal damage. Patient doesn't copy lef…
articles.content:
Disorder / Diagnosis

定義

Failure to attend to contralateral (usually left) space, typically after right parietal damage. Patient doesn't copy left side of drawing, eats only right side of plate, denies left arm exists. Distinct from blindness — is a failure of attention, not vision.

記憶口訣

💡 Neglect = the brain saying Not Existing Left. Right parietal damage makes left space invisible to attention.

考試關鍵字

right parietal, contralateral, spatial attention, line bisection, drawing task, Bálint's syndrome, anosognosia, extinction

articles.content:
Disorder / Diagnosis

定義

Progressive neurodegenerative disorder caused by loss of dopaminergic neurons in substantia nigra pars compacta, reducing striatal dopamine. Motor symptoms: tremor at rest, rigidity, bradykinesia, postural instability. MPTP model in monkeys. Treatment: L-dopa, deep brain stimulation (STN/GPi).

記憶口訣

💡 Parkinson's = Paralyzed by dopamine loss. TRAP: Tremor at rest, Rigidity, Akinesia/bradykinesia, Postural instability. Treat with L-dopa (dopamine precursor).

考試關鍵字

substantia nigra, dopamine, bradykinesia, rigidity, tremor, MPTP, L-dopa, deep brain stimulation, basal ganglia, nigrostriatal pathway

相關人物

👤 Langston, Schultz

📚 Gazzaniga et al., Cognitive Neuroscience 5e · p. 361-363

articles.title: Phineas Gage Case
articles.summary: Classic 1848 case: railroad worker survived iron rod through left frontal lobe (specifically vmPFC/OFC). Before: respons…
articles.content:
Key Figure / Theorist

定義

Classic 1848 case: railroad worker survived iron rod through left frontal lobe (specifically vmPFC/OFC). Before: responsible and well-liked. After: profane, socially inappropriate, unable to plan. Damasio: vmPFC damage disrupts somatic markers needed for social decision-making. Preserved language and IQ.

記憶口訣

💡 Phineas Gage = Personality Gone after frontal lobe damage. Smart but socially shattered. The vmPFC connects emotion to decision-making.

考試關鍵字

vmPFC, OFC, personality change, social decision-making, tamping rod, frontal lobe, Damasio, somatic marker, social behavior

相關人物

👤 Harlow (case), Damasio (interpretation)

📚 Gazzaniga et al., Cognitive Neuroscience 5e · p. 567-568

articles.title: Planum Temporale Asymmetry
articles.summary: Triangular region on upper surface of superior temporal gyrus (posterior Heschl's). Larger in left hemisphere (67% of pe…
articles.content:
Biological Mechanism

定義

Triangular region on upper surface of superior temporal gyrus (posterior Heschl's). Larger in left hemisphere (67% of people, including most right-handers). Corresponds anatomically to Wernicke's area. Proposed as anatomical basis for left hemisphere language dominance. Found in some non-human primates.

考試關鍵字

planum temporale, temporal lobe asymmetry, Wernicke's area, left hemisphere, language dominance, Geschwind, Levitsky, auditory cortex

👤 Geschwind, Levitsky

articles.title: Prefrontal Cortex (PFC)
articles.summary: Anterior frontal lobe. Roles: working memory (dlPFC), decision-making (vmPFC/OFC), cognitive control, goal-directed beha…
articles.content:
Biological Mechanism

定義

Anterior frontal lobe. Roles: working memory (dlPFC), decision-making (vmPFC/OFC), cognitive control, goal-directed behavior, inhibition of action. Phineas Gage: vmPFC damage -> personality change. Matures last (~25 years). Goldman-Rakic: PFC and working memory.

記憶口訣

💡 PFC = Planning, Flexibility, Control. The brain's CEO — last to mature, first to show personality when damaged.

考試關鍵字

working memory, cognitive control, dlPFC, vmPFC, OFC, Phineas Gage, decision making, goal-oriented behavior, maturation

相關人物

👤 Goldman-Rakic, Damasio, Milner

📚 Gazzaniga et al., Cognitive Neuroscience 5e · p. 516-526

articles.title: Primary Motor Cortex (M1) and Motor Homunculus
articles.summary: Precentral gyrus (BA4). Somatotopic organization (motor homunculus: Penfield). Corticospinal tract originates here. Geor…
articles.content:
Biological Mechanism

定義

Precentral gyrus (BA4). Somatotopic organization (motor homunculus: Penfield). Corticospinal tract originates here. Georgopoulos: neurons show directional tuning, population vectors code movement direction. Damage causes contralateral spastic paralysis.

記憶口訣

💡 M1 = Movement maker. Homunculus: face at bottom, feet at top of precentral gyrus. Hands get huge representation.

考試關鍵字

motor homunculus, Penfield, BA4, precentral gyrus, corticospinal tract, directional tuning, population vector, Georgopoulos, somatotopy

相關人物

👤 Penfield, Georgopoulos

📚 Gazzaniga et al., Cognitive Neuroscience 5e · p. 331-334

articles.title: Priming (Perceptual and Conceptual)
articles.summary: Prior exposure to a stimulus facilitates subsequent processing of that or related stimuli. Perceptual priming: faster id…
articles.content:
Theory

定義

Prior exposure to a stimulus facilitates subsequent processing of that or related stimuli. Perceptual priming: faster identification of physically similar stimuli (visual cortex). Conceptual/semantic priming: faster processing of semantically related concepts (bilateral). Spared in amnesia: does NOT require hippocampus.

考試關鍵字

semantic priming, perceptual priming, implicit memory, spared amnesia, repetition priming, facilitation, lexical decision, word fragment completion

相關人物

👤 Tulving, Schacter

📚 Gazzaniga et al., Cognitive Neuroscience 5e · p. 392-394

articles.title: Procedural Memory (Nondeclarative Memory)
articles.summary: Implicit, non-conscious memory for skills and habits (riding a bike, mirror tracing), classical conditioning, and primin…
articles.content:
Theory

定義

Implicit, non-conscious memory for skills and habits (riding a bike, mirror tracing), classical conditioning, and priming. Does NOT depend on hippocampus. Spared in H.M. Depends on basal ganglia (habits), cerebellum (motor skills), and amygdala (fear conditioning).

記憶口訣

💡 Procedural = Proceed without thinking. Your hands know how to type even when you can't explain it. H.M. could learn mirror tracing!

考試關鍵字

implicit memory, skills, habits, classical conditioning, priming, basal ganglia, cerebellum, H.M. spared, mirror tracing

articles.title: Prosopagnosia
articles.summary: Inability to recognize faces despite intact visual acuity. Associated with damage to right fusiform face area (fusiform …
articles.content:
Disorder / Diagnosis

定義

Inability to recognize faces despite intact visual acuity. Associated with damage to right fusiform face area (fusiform gyrus). Can be acquired (stroke) or developmental (congenital). Patients recognize by voice, gait, clothing. Cannot recognize famous faces or own face in mirror.

記憶口訣

💡 Prosopagnosia = Prosopon (face) + agnosia (not knowing). Can't recognize FACES. Think Brad Pitt couldn't recognize Brad Pitt.

articles.title: Reward Prediction Error
articles.summary: Dopamine neurons fire for unexpected rewards (positive prediction error), are suppressed by expected rewards that don't …
articles.content:
Theory

定義

Dopamine neurons fire for unexpected rewards (positive prediction error), are suppressed by expected rewards that don't occur (negative prediction error), and don't respond to fully expected rewards. Schultz's temporal difference learning model. Key mechanism of reinforcement learning in basal ganglia.

記憶口訣

💡 Prediction Error: DA fires for SURPRISE reward. Expected reward = no DA response. Worse than expected = DA DROPS below baseline.

考試關鍵字

Schultz, dopamine, temporal difference learning, reward, punishment, VTA, striatum, reinforcement learning, wanting vs liking, Berridge

相關人物

articles.summary: The ability to selectively process one stimulus or location while ignoring others. Two varieties: voluntary (endogenous,…

The ability to selectively process one stimulus or location while ignoring others. Two varieties: voluntary (endogenous, top-down, goal-driven) and reflexive (exogenous, bottom-up, stimulus-driven). Dorsal attention network (voluntary) vs. ventral attention network (reflexive reorienting).

💡 Selective Attention = spotlight on ONE thing. Voluntary (TOP-down you choose) vs Reflexive (BOTTOM-up something grabs you).

Research Method

Single dissociation: damage to area A impairs task X but not Y (suggests A contributes to X). Double dissociation: damage to A impairs X not Y; damage to B impairs Y not X. Double dissociation is stronger evidence for functional independence of two processes.

記憶口訣

💡 Double dissociation = Double proof. A breaks X not Y; B breaks Y not X. Now we're SURE they're separate.

Theory

定義

Dorsal Attention Network (DAN): IPS + FEF, voluntary/endogenous top-down control of spatial attention. Ventral Attention Network (VAN): right TPJ + right VFC, reflexive/exogenous reorienting to unexpected stimuli. Corbetta and Shulman model. Damage to right VAN causes neglect. Two networks interact.

💡 DAN = Deliberate Attention Network (you choose where to look). VAN = Vigilance Alert Network (something grabs you). Right VAN damage = left neglect.

Biological Mechanism

定義

V1 (primary visual cortex, BA17): retinotopic map, orientation columns, simple and complex cells (Hubel & Wiesel). V2/V3: spatial frequency, depth. V4: color processing; damage = achromatopsia. MT/V5: motion perception; damage = akinetopsia (can't see movement).

記憶口訣

💡 V4 = Color (4 letters = hue). MT/V5 = Motion (M for Movement). No MT = can't see Motion (akinetopsia).

考試關鍵字

V1, V4, MT/V5, retinotopic map, orientation tuning, ocular dominance columns, achromatopsia, akinetopsia, Hubel, Wiesel

相關人物

👤 Hubel, Wiesel, Zeki

📚 Gazzaniga et al., Cognitive Neuroscience 5e · p. 192-200

articles.title: Wernicke's Area
articles.summary: Left posterior superior temporal gyrus (BA 22). Critical for language comprehension and word recognition. Damage causes …
articles.content:
Biological Mechanism

定義

Left posterior superior temporal gyrus (BA 22). Critical for language comprehension and word recognition. Damage causes Wernicke's aphasia: fluent but meaningless speech (word salad) with poor comprehension. Part of left perisylvian language network.

記憶口訣

💡 Wernicke's = WORDS without MEANING. Fluent but nonsensical. Can produce speech but can't understand it.

考試關鍵字

BA 22, language comprehension, word recognition, Wernicke's aphasia, fluent aphasia, word salad, neologisms, paraphasias

articles.title: Wernicke-Lichtheim Model of Language
articles.summary: Classic connectionist model of language. Wernicke's area = auditory word images; Broca's area = motor speech patterns. C…
articles.content:
Theory

定義

Classic connectionist model of language. Wernicke's area = auditory word images; Broca's area = motor speech patterns. Connection between them (arcuate fasciculus) = repetition. Lesion at each node or connection predicts different aphasia type. Geschwind updated with disconnection syndromes.

考試關鍵字

Wernicke, Broca, arcuate fasciculus, disconnection, aphasia classification, Lichtheim, Geschwind, conduction aphasia, language model

articles.title: Working Memory
articles.summary: Active short-term maintenance and manipulation of information. Baddeley's model: phonological loop (verbal), visuospatia…
articles.content:
Theory

定義

Active short-term maintenance and manipulation of information. Baddeley's model: phonological loop (verbal), visuospatial sketchpad (visual), episodic buffer (integrates), central executive (controls). Depends on dlPFC. Capacity ~4 items (not Miller's magical 7 plus/minus 2).

記憶口訣

💡 Working Memory = RAM in the brain. Baddeley's 4 components: Phonological Loop + Visuospatial Sketchpad + Episodic Buffer + Central Executive.

考試關鍵字

Baddeley, phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, central executive, episodic buffer, PFC, dlPFC, capacity, Miller's 7

相關人物

👤 Baddeley, Goldman-Rakic, Miller

📚 Gazzaniga et al., Cognitive Neuroscience 5e · p. 387-389

articles.title: Working Memory (Baddeley Model)
articles.summary: Baddeley and Hitch (1974): Working memory has three components: (1) Central Executive (attentional controller), (2) Phon…
articles.content:
Theory

定義

Baddeley and Hitch (1974): Working memory has three components: (1) Central Executive (attentional controller), (2) Phonological Loop (verbal/acoustic storage and rehearsal), (3) Visuospatial Sketchpad (visual and spatial information). Later added episodic buffer. Prefrontal cortex critical for working memory maintenance.

記憶口訣

💡 Working memory = CEO (Central Executive) + phone (Phonological Loop) + sketch (Visuospatial Sketchpad). Think: the CEO uses a phone and a sketchpad.

考試關鍵字

Baddeley, central executive, phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, episodic buffer, Miller magic number 7, prefrontal cortex, short-term memory

相關人物

👤 Baddeley, Hitch, Goldman-Rakic

📚 Gazzaniga, Cognitive Neuroscience 5th ed. · p. 387-389 (Gazzaniga)

articles.title: fMRI (Functional MRI) Principles
articles.summary: Measures BOLD (blood oxygen level-dependent) signal as proxy for neural activity. When neurons fire, local blood flow in…
articles.content:
Research Method

定義

Measures BOLD (blood oxygen level-dependent) signal as proxy for neural activity. When neurons fire, local blood flow increases (hemodynamic response). Superior spatial resolution vs. EEG/MEG. Poor temporal resolution (seconds). Indirect measure. Cannot establish causality alone. Developed by Ogawa (1990), Kwong and Raichle.

記憶口訣

💡 fMRI = Fired neurons Need More blood. BOLD = Blood Oxygen Level Dependent. Good SPACE, bad TIME. Cannot prove causation (use TMS for that).

考試關鍵字

BOLD signal, hemodynamic response, spatial resolution, temporal resolution, indirect measure, deoxyhemoglobin, Ogawa, fMRI limitations, neuroimaging

相關人物

👤 Ogawa, Kwong, Raichle, Fox, Petersen

📚 Gazzaniga, Cognitive Neuroscience 5th ed. · p. 106-111 (Gazzaniga)

articles.title: fMRI (Functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging)
articles.summary: Neuroimaging method measuring BOLD (blood oxygen level-dependent) signal. Detects changes in oxygenated vs. deoxygenated…
articles.content:
Research Method

定義

Neuroimaging method measuring BOLD (blood oxygen level-dependent) signal. Detects changes in oxygenated vs. deoxygenated hemoglobin as a proxy for neural activity. High spatial resolution (~mm), poor temporal resolution (~seconds). Most widely used human neuroimaging.

記憶口訣

💡 fMRI = Finding Metabolic Responses Indirectly. Measures blood O2 changes, not neurons directly.

考試關鍵字

BOLD, hemoglobin, spatial resolution, temporal resolution, hemodynamic response, block design, event-related design, limitations

相關人物

👤 Ogawa, Kwong, Thulborn, Raichle

📚 Gazzaniga et al., Cognitive Neuroscience 5e · p. 106-111
